recommendations: add a minimal smoke-test suite (import tests + one prompt-to-image dry run with mocked weights) and wire it into CI
fix packaging metadata detection by consolidating to a modern `pyproject.toml` (or complete `setup.cfg`) with explicit runtime/optional extras and console entry points
split `tools/train/train.py` and `src/dalle_mini/model/modeling.py` into smaller modules (config/loading
train loop
eval
checkpointing; attention/block/heads) to reduce complexity and improve maintainability
remove duplicated inference backend logic between Gradio and Streamlit by introducing a shared `app/common/inference.py`
add strict typing + linting + formatting gates (mypy/pyright
ruff
black) and pre-commit hooks
create deterministic reproducibility controls (global seeding
pinned dependency ranges
documented JAX/Flax/CUDA matrix)
add robust error handling and user-facing validation in `get_images_from_prompt` (empty prompts
max length
OOM/timeouts
graceful fallbacks)
implement lightweight benchmark scripts for inference latency/memory and training throughput with tracked baselines
add data-pipeline validation tests for `Dataset/DatasetConfig` (schema checks
corrupted sample handling
shard edge cases)
improve observability with structured logging and optional W&B/metrics toggles for both training and app serving
harden release workflows by adding versioning/changelog automation and CI checks for wheel/sdist build + install + import
add API-level docs for exposed MCP endpoints (inputs/outputs/examples) and de-duplicate overlapping endpoint names
introduce caching layers for tokenizer/processor/model initialization in app backends to reduce cold-start cost
add security/dependency hygiene checks (pip-audit/safety
Dependabot/Renovate) and pin critical transitive deps
include contribution guidelines with architecture diagrams and “good first issues” to reduce onboarding friction

success_factors: Workflow reached terminal status `success` and executed all planned nodes: download, analysis, env, generate, run, review, finalize.
Resilience mechanism worked: after git clone timeout, pipeline continued via zip fallback (`processed_by: zip_fallback`, success true).
MCP plugin scaffolding was generated successfully with runnable entrypoint (`start_mcp.py`) and healthy service status over stdio.
Import-based adapter strategy matched repository characteristics (Python package with identifiable model/data/app modules).
failure_reasons: Primary source acquisition failed in DownloadNode due to network/RPC timeout (`curl 28 Recv failure`, `expected flush after ref listing`).
Repository/dependency detection fields in one analysis block were inconsistent (reported no pyproject/setup despite files existing), indicating metadata parsing reliability issues.
Quality telemetry was incomplete (`llm_calls=0`, `total_files_processed=0`, `tool_endpoints=0` in summary) despite actual generated endpoints, reducing trust in reported metrics.

node_performance.download_time: Download node experienced a blocking failure on git clone; likely dominant delay contributor before fallback activation.
node_performance.analysis_time: Analysis completed successfully via zip-imported content (35 files), including module and CLI mapping; moderate efficiency for a complex repo.
node_performance.generation_time: Generation succeeded quickly enough to complete full run in 372s, producing standard MCP file set and requirements.
node_performance.test_time: Original project tests did not pass/execute meaningfully, while MCP plugin health checks passed; test depth is minimal and startup timing metrics are missing.
